text:
Once upon a time in a lively village, there lived a peculiar old owl named Old Winn. Winn was unusual. Instead of laying eggs, he hatched from a golden egg on Sundays. He was known for his odd habit of collecting tiny pebbles. Every morning as the sun peeked over the mountains, he would wobble down from his nest among the ancient oaks to pick up a sparkler of a rock.

Winnie noticed something strange. Despite the variety of rocks he collected, they never seemed to fit together perfectly. He pondered this mystery until one evening, sitting under a moonlit willow.

An idea struck him. Perhaps there was a way to tell stories with his rocks. He sat down on a pile of pebbles he'd gathered and began to weave tales.

One rock spun into a tale of bravery in a forest fire. Another told of a lost queen searching for her beloved. And so forth. His stories continued through the night until every pebble
